data.name: zone_dict
data.description: **PRE-DEFINED VARIABLE (dict)**: Dictionary mapping zone_id to zone infrastructure information. Use zone_dict to find which bus stops belong to specific zones via the 'bus_stops' field. See zone_info.json for full documentation. Use directly: `zone_dict`, NOT `zone_dict()`.

relevant_fields.bus_stops: List of bus stop IDs in this zone
relevant_fields.subway_stations: List of subway station IDs in this zone (for transfer analysis)

data.usage_example:
```python
# Find bus stops in a specific zone
zone_id = '2_5'
if zone_id in zone_dict:
    bus_stops = zone_dict[zone_id]['bus_stops']
    print(f"Bus stops in zone {zone_id}: {bus_stops}")

# Find zones with transit stations
transit_zones = [zid for zid, info in zone_dict.items() 
                 if info['bus_stops'] or info['subway_stations']]
```

data.name: bus_route_info
data.description: Dictionary containing detailed bus route information including static structure (stations, segments, zones served) and real-time operational data (current headway, active buses, delays). Updated during simulation runs. See zone_info.json for full documentation.

route_id.edges: List[str] - ordered list of edge IDs along the route
route_id.total_length_m: float - total route length in meters
route_id.scheduled_runtime_s: float - planned end-to-end runtime in seconds
route_id.stations: List of station info dicts with station_id, zone_id, position_m, stop_index
route_id.segments: List of segment info dicts with from_station, to_station, length_m, scheduled_travel_time_s
route_id.zones_served: List[str] - list of zone IDs the route passes through
route_id.transfer_stations: List of transfer station info with connecting_routes and connecting_subway_lines

realtime.active_buses: List[str] - vehicle IDs currently in service
realtime.segment_status: List of segment status with current_travel_time_s, delay_s, buses_in_segment
realtime.station_load: List of station load with waiting_passengers, boarding_rate, alighting_rate
realtime.last_update_step: int - simulation step of last update

data.usage_example:
```python
# Get route information
route_id = 'route_bus1_eastbound'
if route_id in bus_route_info:
    route = bus_route_info[route_id]
    print(f"Route {route['route_name']}: {route['total_length_m']:.0f}m")
    print(f"Zones served: {route['zones_served']}")
    
    # Check real-time data
    rt = route['realtime']
    print(f"Active buses: {rt['bus_count']}")
    print(f"Current headway: {rt['current_headway_s']}s")
    
    # Find delayed segments
    for seg in rt['segment_status']:
        if seg['delay_s'] > 60:
            print(f"Delay on {seg['from_station']} -> {seg['to_station']}: {seg['delay_s']}s")
```

data.name: transit_graph
data.description: NetworkX DiGraph representing the public transit network including bus and subway routes and stations. Nodes are either routes or stations, edges represent service relationships. See zone_info.json for full documentation.

nodes.station_id: Bus stop or subway station identifier (node_type='station')

data.usage_example:
```python
# Find all stations served by a route
route_id = 'route_bus1_eastbound'
stations = [n for n in transit_graph.successors(route_id) 
            if transit_graph.nodes[n].get('node_type') == 'station']

# Find routes serving a station
station_id = 'stop_bus1_0_east'
routes = [n for n in transit_graph.predecessors(station_id)
          if transit_graph.nodes[n].get('node_type') == 'route']
```

data.name: current_bus_schedule
data.description: **PRE-DEFINED VARIABLE (dict)**: Dictionary containing the current bus scheduling configuration for all routes with time-based scheduling support. CRITICAL: (1) Use copy.deepcopy() to copy structure - DO NOT manually reconstruct. (2) DO NOT MODIFY station_id or time_range values. (3) ONLY modify headway and dwell_time values. Use directly: `current_bus_schedule`, NOT `current_bus_schedule()`.

timetable.time_range: [start_time, end_time] in seconds (list of 2 ints). Time periods within an hour for different scheduling strategies
timetable.headway: time between buses in seconds (int). CONSTRAINTS: min=60 (1 min), max=600 (10 min), default=180 (3 min)

schedule.dwell_time: time spent at station in seconds (int). CONSTRAINTS: min=10, max=60, default=20

time_range.description: Time periods in seconds within simulation. Multiple time ranges allow different headways for peak/off-peak hours. Time ranges must not overlap and should cover the entire simulation period. WARNING: DO NOT MODIFY time_range values when creating new configurations - always copy them exactly from current_bus_schedule.

headway.description: Minimum 60 seconds (1 min) to ensure safe bus separation. Maximum 600 seconds (10 min) to maintain reasonable service frequency.

dwell_time.description: Minimum 10 seconds for passenger boarding/alighting. Maximum 60 seconds to avoid excessive delays.

functions.name: read_bus_states
functions.description: Query historical bus operation data. Returns a DICT with keys: 'snapshots', 'metadata', 'total_snapshots'. Access snapshots via data['snapshots']. Each station in snapshot.bus.lines[line_id].stations[station_id] has 'waiting_count' (int), 'avg_waiting_time', 'max_waiting_time', 'waiting_time_distribution'. DO NOT use open() or json.load() directly. Automatically uses the most recent traffic states file.

input_format.start_time: Optional start time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.end_time: Optional end time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.exact_time: Optional exact time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.line_id: Optional filter by specific bus route (str, default: None)
input_format.max_snapshots: Optional maximum number of snapshots to return (int, default: None for all)

metadata.file_path: str - path to traffic states file

line_id.active_buses: int - number of buses currently running on this route
line_id.station_count: int - number of stations on this route

bus_id.next_station_dwell_time: float - planned dwell time at next station (seconds)

station_id.waiting_count: int - number of passengers waiting at station

waiting_time_distribution.0-60s: int - count of passengers waiting 0-60 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.60-180s: int - count of passengers waiting 60-180 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.180-300s: int - count of passengers waiting 180-300 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.>300s: int - count of passengers waiting over 300 seconds

structure.total_snapshots: int - total number of snapshots returned
